title: TC4x- ADC ASIL-B and ASIL-D use cases

content.text: Overview
This page is to analyze the ADC use cases for ASIL-B and ASIL-D applications
Use cases
ASIL-D Use cases
Use case 1: Diverse redundant ADC channels

The MCU receives independent redundant signals from the external analog device(s) which are processed by redundant and independent resources of the MCU. Each analog input signal processing result can be read by a bus master like for example CPU, DMA or PPU.
In this Use Case, 2 options of ADC diverse redundancy are available
Not all AURIX TC4xx devices feature the DSADC or FCC converters. Please refer to the device’s datasheet for more information on the available converters

content.local_path: _images/TC4x- ADC ASIL-B and ASIL-D use cases/Diverse redundant ADC channels.jpg

content.text: SMs needed for this use case

items: If the redundant ADC converters share a common VAREF input, then the system integrator shall ensure that the external safety mechanism shown below is implemented

ESM[SW]:ADC:RESULT_PLAUSIBILITY → However, if SM[HW]:ADC:RESULT_PLAUSIBILITY is used to perform plausibility checks on the signal properties, then these checks do not have to be implemented in software again

content.text: Use case 2: Homogenous redundant ADC channels
Which is same as above, but instead of diverse ADC units here we use same ADC conversion type units but with different MCU resources
2 options of ADC homogeneous redundancy are available
ASIL-B Use cases
Single input redundant ADC channels

The MCU receives one signal from the external analog device. The signal, that is acquired through one single package pin, is then branched inside the MCU to two redundant and independent ADC converter modules (shared package pin). Each of the redundant module's process then the shared signal. Each analog signal processing result can be read by bus master
In this use case, 3 different options of ADC redundancy are available

content.local_path: _images/TC4x- ADC ASIL-B and ASIL-D use cases/Homogenous redundant ADC channels.jpg

content.text: SMs needed for this use case
ESM[SW]:ADC:BROKEN_WIRE_DETECTION → New SM added for this case
ESM[SW]:ADC:MODULE_REDUNDANCY
ESM[SW]:ADC:ISR_MONITOR
ESM[SW]:ADC:VAREF_PLAUSIBILITY
SM[HW]:ADC:RESULT_PLAUSIBILITY
Single input single ADC channel
The MCU receives a single analog signal from an external analog device which is processed by a single TMADC channel

content.local_path: _images/TC4x- ADC ASIL-B and ASIL-D use cases/Single input single ADC channel.jpg

content.text: SMs needed for this use case
ESM[SW]:ADC:BROKEN_WIRE_DETECTION
ESM[SW]:ADC:CHANNEL_CORE_SELF_DIAGNOSIS - added for this use case
ESM[SW]:ADC:ISR_MONITOR
ESM[SW]:ADC:VAREF_PLAUSIBILITY
SM[HW]:ADC:RESULT_PLAUSIBILIT
